issues.title: Seed task board from open labeled issues
issues.body:
## Goal

Seed task board from open labeled issues

## Evidence

File .github/ai-state/task-board.json does not exist. readFacts returns null for this path.

## Scope

Task type: execution

Rationale: task-board.json does not exist. The self-cycle cannot project task state without a task board.

Readiness: No task-board.json found.

## Acceptance

- `npm run check` passes

## Constraints

- Stay within allowed files.
- Do not edit forbidden files.

## Rollback / Follow-up

If seeding produces incorrect task entries, delete task-board.json and re-seed from the corrected issue list. Follow up by verifying the task board matches open labeled issues.

---

issues.title: Experiment: Adding a failure reflection step to classify-self-cycle-failure.js that generates actionable self-critiques reduces repeat failures
issues.body:
## Goal
Opportunity signal opp-a1b2c3d4-004: Adding a failure reflection step to classify-self-cycle-failure.js that generates actionable self-critiques reduces repeat failures. Reasoning: Current failure classification produces categories but not actionable lessons. Reflexion-style self-critiques stored in meta-signals would prevent repeating the same mistakes
## Evidence
Source facts: Reflexion agents improve by reflecting on failures and storing self-critiques. Expected impact: repeat-failure-rate from unknown to < 10% repeat rate.
## Rollback / Follow-up
Revert changes if experiment fails success criteria.

---

issues.title: Experiment: Adding Claude Code hooks for forbiddenFiles enforcement provides defense-in-depth against constitution violations
issues.body:
## Goal
Opportunity signal opp-a1b2c3d4-003: Adding Claude Code hooks for forbiddenFiles enforcement provides defense-in-depth against constitution violations. Reasoning: Currently seed-constitution rules are enforced only at task-contract level. Hooks would catch violations at the tool-call level
## Evidence
Source facts: Claude Code hooks enable pre/post execution guards on tool calls. Expected impact: constitution-violation-rate from 0 (task-contract enforcement) to 0 (defense-in-depth).
## Rollback / Follow-up
Revert changes if experiment fails success criteria.

---

issues.title: Experiment: Adding a structured memory retrieval layer to meta-signals improves issue production quality
issues.body:
## Goal
Opportunity signal opp-a1b2c3d4-002: Adding a structured memory retrieval layer to meta-signals improves issue production quality. Reasoning: Current meta-signals.json is a flat dump. MemGPT-style tiered memory with relevance ranking would help the issue producer focus on the most impactful gaps
## Evidence
Source facts: MemGPT tiered memory: working, archival, episodic with active management. Expected impact: issue-production-relevance from generic gap detection to context-aware gap prioritization.
## Rollback / Follow-up
Revert changes if experiment fails success criteria.

---

issues.title: Experiment: Explicit state machine transitions for worker lifecycle reduce status inconsistency bugs
issues.body:
## Goal
Opportunity signal opp-a1b2c3d4-001: Explicit state machine transitions for worker lifecycle reduce status inconsistency bugs. Reasoning: LangGraph DAG model makes transitions explicit and auditable, unlike current free-form status field updates
## Evidence
Source facts: LangGraph uses DAG-based state machines for agent workflows. Expected impact: worker-status-consistency from occasional stale status to zero stale status.
## Rollback / Follow-up
Revert changes if experiment fails success criteria.

---

issues.title: Investigate: structured-memory-retrieval
issues.body:
## Goal
External research (external-doc): MemGPT implements a tiered memory system for agents: in-context memory (working), archival memory (long-term), and recall memory (episodic). The agent actively manages its own memory, deciding what to store, retrieve, and forget. This enables coherent behavior over extended interactions. Relevance: LIAN meta-signals.json and fact-events.ndjson provide raw memory but no retrieval or prioritization layer. Adding a structured memory system that ranks facts by relevance to the current task could improve decision quality in issue production and worker dispatch.
## Evidence
Source reliability: medium. Captured at: 2026-05-13T04:17:06.933Z. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.11584.
## Rollback / Follow-up
If the investigation finds no actionable improvement, close the issue with a summary of findings.

---

issues.title: Investigate: function-routing-between-agents
issues.body:
## Goal
External research (external-doc): Swarm uses function calling to route tasks between specialized agents. Each agent exposes handoff functions that transfer context to another agent. No central orchestrator 鈥� agents self-route based on the current context and their capabilities. Relevance: LIAN Command Steward acts as a central orchestrator. A hybrid approach where workers can hand off subtasks to other workers (within file-scope bounds) could reduce Command Steward bottleneck while maintaining safety through the existing conflict-group system.
## Evidence
Source reliability: medium. Captured at: 2026-05-13T04:17:06.807Z. Source: https://github.com/openai/swarm.
## Rollback / Follow-up
If the investigation finds no actionable improvement, close the issue with a summary of findings.

---

issues.title: Investigate: delegation-and-oversight
issues.body:
## Goal
External research (external-doc): CrewAI assigns agents specific roles (researcher, writer, reviewer) with explicit delegation chains. A manager agent can delegate subtasks to specialized agents, each with their own tools and constraints. Supports sequential, parallel, and hierarchical execution modes. Relevance: LIAN workers have role packets but no delegation chain. A worker cannot spawn a sub-worker for a specialized subtask. Adding bounded sub-worker delegation (with the same conflict-group and file-scope constraints) could improve task decomposition quality.
## Evidence
Source reliability: high. Captured at: 2026-05-13T04:17:06.687Z. Source: https://github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI.
## Rollback / Follow-up
If the investigation finds no actionable improvement, close the issue with a summary of findings.

---

issues.title: Investigate: reflexion-self-reflection
issues.body:
## Goal
External research (external-doc): Reflexion agents improve by reflecting on failures: they generate self-critiques after task failures, store them in episodic memory, and reference them in subsequent attempts. This creates a feedback loop where the agent learns from its own mistakes without weight updates. Relevance: LIAN classify-self-cycle-failure.js classifies failures but does not generate actionable self-critiques for future cycles. Adding a reflection step that stores failure analysis in meta-signals would enable the system to avoid repeating the same mistakes.
## Evidence
Source reliability: medium. Captured at: 2026-05-13T04:17:06.560Z. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.11432.
## Rollback / Follow-up
If the investigation finds no actionable improvement, close the issue with a summary of findings.

---

issues.title: Investigate: event-driven-guardrails
issues.body:
## Goal
External research (external-doc): Claude Code hooks allow pre/post execution guards on tool calls. Can enforce file-scope restrictions, validate outputs before commit, and trigger side effects (notifications, audits) on specific events. Relevance: LIAN seed-constitution rules are enforced at the task-contract level but not at the tool-call level. Adding Claude Code hooks for forbiddenFiles enforcement would provide defense-in-depth against constitution violations.
## Evidence
Source reliability: authoritative. Captured at: 2026-05-13T04:17:06.451Z. Source: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks.
## Rollback / Follow-up
If the investigation finds no actionable improvement, close the issue with a summary of findings.

---

issues.title: Investigate: conversational-agent-collaboration
issues.body:
## Goal
External research (external-doc): AutoGen enables multi-agent collaboration through structured conversations. Agents negotiate task allocation, share context through message history, and can request human feedback at decision points. Supports nested conversations for subtask decomposition. Relevance: LIAN workers currently operate in isolation with no inter-worker communication. Adding a lightweight message bus for conflict resolution and context sharing could reduce duplicate work and improve coordination.
## Evidence
Source reliability: high. Captured at: 2026-05-13T04:17:06.322Z. Source: https://github.com/microsoft/autogen.
## Rollback / Follow-up
If the investigation finds no actionable improvement, close the issue with a summary of findings.

---

issues.title: Investigate: graph-based-state-machine
issues.body:
## Goal
External research (external-doc): LangGraph uses directed acyclic graphs to model agent workflows, enabling explicit state transitions, parallel branching, and conditional routing. Each node is a processing step, edges define transitions. Supports checkpointing and human-in-the-loop interrupts. Relevance: LIAN batch-launch wave planning uses a similar DAG concept for conflict-group serialization. Could adopt explicit state machine transitions for worker lifecycle (planned->running->completed/failed) instead of status field patching.
## Evidence
Source reliability: high. Captured at: 2026-05-13T04:16:40.745Z. Source: https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph.
## Rollback / Follow-up
If the investigation finds no actionable improvement, close the issue with a summary of findings.

---
